# Ceòlmhor — Full Content Mirror

> Edinburgh-based Highland bagpipe tuition and performance. Scottish Excellence · Piping Tuition and Performance.
> Contact: contact@ceolmhor.scot | Edinburgh, Scotland | https://www.ceolmhor.scot

---

## Home

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/

Ceòlmhor ("kyowl-vor" · melodious) — Scottish Excellence · Piping Tuition and Performance. Based in Edinburgh, Scotland. Teaching and performing worldwide.

### What We Offer

**Performance**
Weddings, funerals, Burns Nights, Hogmanay, corporate dinners, military and civic ceremonies. Each booking comes with clear written terms, a full repertoire discussion, and pricing from £125. Every occasion is different. A bespoke piece can be composed and premiered at your event — something that will not be played anywhere else.

**Tuition**
Four progressive tiers, from the first notes on the practice chanter through to competition-standard Pìobaireachd. Lessons are structured, cumulative, and taught in Edinburgh or online worldwide. The first lesson is complimentary — no charge, no obligation to continue.

**Compositions**
A growing catalogue of original pieces — marches, airs, and Pìobaireachd — available for performance and download. Commissions accepted for personal milestones, pipe bands, and institutional or heritage engagements.

### Key Prices
- Tuition from £30 per hour
- Performance Packages from £125
- First Lesson Complimentary

### Venues, Institutions & Events Teams
Professional Scottish piping for hotels, private venues, universities, cultural organisations, civic occasions and corporate events. Ceòlmhor can support guest arrivals, formal welcomes, ceremonies, dinner entrances, Burns Suppers and public-facing occasions.

### Testimonials
- "The pipes brought the whole evening together in a way nothing else could have. An exceptional performance — the music was perfectly chosen and impeccably played." — Alex Moir-Barnetson, Ceilidh, January 2026
- "I had no experience whatsoever when I started. The structure of the lessons made everything feel achievable from the very first session. I would not have progressed half as quickly without that framework." — Molly Reay, Tuition Student, February 2026
- "The pipes at our corporate dinner were the talking point of the night. Professional from first contact to final note. Exactly what the occasion needed." — Struan Macdonald, Corporate Client, March 2026

---

## About

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/about.html

The Highland bagpipe is one of the few instruments that carries a culture within it — a language, a history, and a ceremonial weight that most tuition never addresses. Ceòlmhor was founded on the conviction that teaching the instrument without teaching that context is to teach it incompletely. The curriculum is built accordingly: structured, progressive, and rooted in the Gàidhlig tradition from the first lesson to the last.

That same conviction extends to performance. Every engagement — whether a wedding ceremony in Edinburgh, a Burns Supper in the Borders, or a corporate occasion anywhere in Scotland — is approached with knowledge of what the music carries and what the occasion demands. Ceòlmhor does not simply provide a piper. It brings the full weight of the tradition to bear on every moment that deserves it.

> "The music and its language were never separate."

### Meet Your Piper — Felix Walker

Piping Instructor & Professional Piper

Felix Walker began on the practice chanter with Fèis Dhùn Èideann — Edinburgh's Gaelic arts festival and tuition organisation — at a time when he was also attending the Gaelic Medium Education primary school in the city. The language and the music arrived together, and he has never thought of them as separate.

The pipes came later — a Christmas present that changed the direction of his musical life. What followed was a balance between school-based tuition, which gave pipe band grounding and a modern outlook, and Fèis tuition, which kept the traditional and Gàidhlig emphasis at the centre. Ceòlmhor grew out of that formation — a teaching practice built on the conviction that the instrument and its tradition are inseparable, and that students deserve to be taught both.

### Credentials

**Armistice Day — Scottish National War Memorial**
Lone Piper at the Armistice Day Service, Scottish National War Memorial, Edinburgh Castle — one of the most ceremonially significant piping engagements in Scotland.

**Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo**
Performed with the Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo from 2024, including the 2026 international tour to Australia and New Zealand — one of the world's most prestigious platforms for Scottish piping.

**Tartan Day Parades**
Selected to perform at the New York Tartan Day Parade and the inaugural Edinburgh Tartan Day Parade — representing Scottish piping tradition on both sides of the Atlantic.

### Collaborations
Ceòlmhor works alongside venues, hotels, cultural spaces, and hospitality brands. Partner organisations include: National Trust for Scotland, Scottish National War Memorial, Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo, The New Club Edinburgh, City of Edinburgh Universities Officers' Training Corps.

---

## Tuition

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/tuition.html

Ceòlmhor's tuition is built around a four-tier curriculum — structured, cumulative, and rooted in the Gàidhlig tradition. Each tier has defined entry and exit standards, so progress is always measurable and the path forward is always clear. Lessons are available in Edinburgh and online worldwide.

### The Curriculum — Four Tiers

**Tier I: Foundational** — https://www.ceolmhor.scot/tuition/tier-1.html
Instrument: Practice Chanter
Embouchure, hand position, the eight-note scale, basic tunes, reading music. Systematic introduction to the mechanics of playing with correct posture and tone from the first lesson.
Exit standard: Can play the scale cleanly. Can play five tunes with sheet music.

**Tier II: Developing** — https://www.ceolmhor.scot/tuition/tier-2.html
Instrument: Practice Chanter
Gracenotes, doublings, repertoire building, introduction to competition pieces. The student develops fluency and begins to build the musical vocabulary required for performance.
Exit standard: Executes all gracenotes consistently. Repertoire of 15 or more tunes.

**Tier III: Proficient** — https://www.ceolmhor.scot/tuition/tier-3.html
Instrument: Bagpipes
Full instrument transition, blowing technique, tuning basics, band repertoire, performance confidence. The physical and technical demands of the full bagpipe are addressed methodically.
Exit standard: Sustains steady pressure. Performs publicly without assistance.

**Tier IV: Advanced** — https://www.ceolmhor.scot/tuition/tier-4.html
Instrument: Bagpipes
MSR, Pìobaireachd introduction, competition-ready pieces, Masterclass format. The student engages with the highest levels of the tradition and is prepared for independent competitive performance.
Exit standard: Competes independently.

### Fees

| Offering | Price |
|---|---|
| Trial Lesson | Free (complimentary first lesson — no obligation) |
| Regular Tuition | £30/hour |
| Block Booking | 10 for 9 (£30 saving) |

Lessons of 30 or 90 minutes can be arranged on request.

Lessons are available in-person in Edinburgh at a mutually agreed location, and via video call for students anywhere in the world. All four tiers are available through both formats. Equipment guidance is provided for students outside the UK.

### What Is Included
- Welcome pack for all new students (sent before first lesson — covers curriculum structure, tier system, and what to expect)
- Progress tracking and written feedback after each lesson (what was covered, what to practise, technique notes)
- Sheet music, reference recordings, and written lesson notes

---

## Performance

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/performance.html

Ceòlmhor performs at weddings, funerals, Burns Nights, corporate dinners, ceilidhs, and civic ceremonies across Edinburgh and Scotland — including the Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo and the Armistice Day Service at the Scottish National War Memorial. Every engagement is handled professionally from first contact to final note.

### Performance Services

| Occasion | Price |
|---|---|
| Wedding & Civil Ceremony | from £225 |
| Funeral & Memorial | from £125 |
| Burns Night | from £200 |
| Corporate & Business Dinner | from £200 |
| Ceilidh & Dance Events | from £200 |
| Hogmanay & the Scottish Calendar | from £200 |
| Custom / Bespoke Engagement | individually agreed |

### Wedding Packages (detail)
- **Ceremony Piper** — £225: arrival music, bridal procession, signing of the register, recessional
- **Reception Piper** — £300: all of the above plus drinks reception piping and photograph background music
- **Full Wedding Piper** — £350: complete day from arrival to grand entrance
- Additional performance set: £75

### Venues & Events Teams
Ceòlmhor works with event teams who need a reliable piper for formal, private and public-facing occasions. This includes hotels, private venues, universities, schools, councils, cultural organisations, charities and corporate hosts. Framework agreements available for recurring and seasonal programmes.

### Location & Travel
Based in Edinburgh. Available across Scotland. Travel surcharge: £0.50 per mile beyond the first five miles from Edinburgh city centre. For destinations requiring overnight accommodation, costs are discussed individually at enquiry stage.

### Booking Terms
- 50% non-refundable deposit required within 7 days of confirmation to secure the date
- Remaining balance due 7 days before the performance
- Travel surcharge: £0.50 per mile beyond the first five miles
- No performance without a signed contract and deposit received
- Written quote valid for 14 days

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/performance/wedding.html

Professional piping for weddings and civil ceremonies in Edinburgh and across Scotland. Full Highland dress worn at all formal engagements. Repertoire discussed and agreed in advance. Book 6–12 months ahead for peak summer weekends.

## Compositions

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/compositions.html

A growing catalogue of original works — marches, slow airs, and Pìobaireachd — available for performance and download. Scores delivered as typeset PDFs. Licensed for personal and private performance use only; commercial recording, broadcast, or competitive performance requires a separate licensing arrangement.

### Commissions

**Private commissions** (individuals and families marking a personal occasion): from £250.

**Event commissions** (composed for and premiered at a performance booking): from £200, in addition to the performance package fee.

**Media and commercial commissions** (film, television, documentary, advertising, branded content): individually agreed.

**Heritage and institutional commissions** (civic bodies, military units, cultural organisations, Burns clubs): individually agreed.

**Commission process:**
1. Submit enquiry via the compositions page commission form — describe the occasion, type of piece, and intended use
2. Brief consultation by phone or email — agree mood, cultural references, and scope
3. Piece composed to agreed brief; reference recording shared for feedback; one round of revisions included
4. Final typeset score and audio recording delivered; licensing terms confirmed in writing

**Timeline:** Standard commissions completed within 4–6 weeks of confirmed brief and receipt of fee.

---

## FAQ

**URL:** https://www.ceolmhor.scot/faq.html

### General

**What is Ceòlmhor?**
An Edinburgh-based Highland bagpipe tuition and performance business founded on the conviction that the instrument cannot be taught or performed without the Gàidhlig culture and tradition that created it. The name means resonant or melodious in Scottish Gaelic — pronounced kyawl-vor.

**Where are you based?**
Edinburgh, Scotland. In-person tuition in Edinburgh. Online tuition worldwide via Zoom. Performance engagements across Scotland and further afield — travel beyond the first five miles from Edinburgh city centre is subject to a surcharge of £0.50 per mile.

**How far do you travel for performances?**
No fixed limit — Highlands, Borders, and beyond Edinburgh all considered. For destinations requiring overnight accommodation, costs are discussed individually at enquiry stage. International engagements accepted.

**How do I make a booking?**
All bookings begin with an enquiry via the contact form. A written quote is issued, valid for 14 days. Once agreed, a written contract is issued and a 50% non-refundable deposit secures the date. The remaining balance is due 7 days before the engagement.

**Do you offer gift vouchers?**
Not currently available but planned. Contact directly for a bespoke arrangement.

**Are you qualified to teach?**
Yes. Ceòlmhor is run by an experienced piping instructor with three years of structured tuition delivery.

### Tuition

**Do I need any experience to start?**
No prior musical experience required. Tuition begins on the practice chanter and progresses to the full Highland bagpipe at Tier III. Bagpipes are not required until Tier III.

**Do I need to buy a practice chanter before the first lesson?**
Yes — required from the first lesson. A basic practice chanter costs between £20 and £50. Guidance on purchasing provided at enrolment.

**How much do lessons cost?**
Trial lesson is complimentary. Regular tuition: £30/hour. Block booking: 10 lessons for the price of 9 (£30 saving). 30- and 90-minute sessions can be arranged on request; 90-minute sessions recommended ahead of competitions or performances.

**How long does it take to learn the bagpipes?**
Most students reach the full Highland bagpipe at Tier III within 1–2 years of consistent weekly lessons. Reaching competition standard at Tier IV typically takes several years. The practice chanter alone is a rewarding instrument and many students enjoy years of progress before transitioning to the full pipes.

**Do you teach children?**
Yes. A parent or guardian must provide written consent before the first lesson, covering data collection, lesson recording policy, and communication channels.

**Do you teach online?**
Yes, worldwide via Zoom. A stable internet connection and a device with camera and microphone are required. Same structured curriculum as in-person lessons, available at all four tiers.

**Cancellation policy for tuition:**
Cancellations with more than 48 hours notice will be rescheduled at no charge. Cancellations within 48 hours are charged at the full lesson rate unless the slot can be filled. Repeated late cancellations may result in the end of the tuition arrangement.

**Do you teach Pìobaireachd?**
Yes — introduced at Tier IV. The oldest and most complex form of pipe music, rooted in the Gàidhlig tradition, taught with full attention to cultural and historical context.

### Performance

**Wedding piper costs in Edinburgh:**
Ceremony Piper: £225 | Reception Piper: £300 | Full Wedding Piper: £350 | Additional set: £75. All subject to travel surcharge beyond 5 miles.

**What will you wear at a performance?**
Full Highland dress at all formal performance engagements.

**Can I request specific tunes?**
Yes. Repertoire requests are discussed and agreed in advance. Full repertoire spans marches, airs, laments, jigs, reels, strathspeys, and Pìobaireachd.

**How far in advance should I book?**
Weddings: 6–12 months in advance. Funerals: short notice always considered as priority. Burns Suppers and corporate events: 2–3 months.

**What happens if you cannot perform on the day?**
Every effort will be made to source a replacement piper of equivalent standard. If no replacement found, a full refund of all monies paid will be issued. Commitment included in the written contract.

**Do you perform outside Scotland?**
Yes. International engagements available. Accommodation and travel costs for destinations beyond driving distance are discussed individually.

**Is a contract required?**
Yes — always. No performance without a signed contract and deposit received.

### Compositions

**Commission costs:** Private commissions from £250. Event commissions from £200 (added to performance booking). Media/commercial and institutional commissions individually agreed.

**Commission timeline:** 4–6 weeks for standard commissions. Institutional timelines agreed individually.

**Can a piece be performed on the night it is commissioned for?**
Yes — event commissions can be premiered at the performance booking. Typeset score and reference recording delivered afterwards.
